cards.back: Covariance matrix estimation can be used to calculate the standard deviation of the difference between the estimated mean salary and the true mean salary, which can be used to estimate the margin of error in the estimate.

Generalized Linear Model (GLM) can be used to model the relationship between salary and other variables such as experience, education, and location, allowing for more accurate estimation of the mean salary.

Fisher’s Linear Discriminant can be used to identify the most relevant variables that contribute to the variation in salary among software engineers, allowing for more targeted and effective estimation of the mean salary.

The estimated mean salary can be used to inform decisions such as salary negotiation, job placement, and career development, allowing software engineers to make more informed choices about their careers.
The margin of error can be used to quantify the uncertainty of the estimated mean salary, allowing for more accurate interpretation of the results and more effective decision-making.
